ops.title: Python style guide for guard hooks
ops.body:
# Python style guide for guard hooks

The canonical guide is the committed `plugin/hooks/STYLEGUIDE.md` (added alongside this doc; pack rules cite its § Type Annotations and § Code Organization). What this record adds is the guard-pack-specific delta — the principles that are NOT generic Python style and are easy to violate when arriving from other Python codebases:

- **Never a wrong allow, never a wrong rewrite.** A guard admits only what it can prove bounded; a rewrite emits only what it can prove semantics-identical (e.g. every regex-dialect acceptance must mean the same thing in GNU grep and Rust regex). Ambiguous shapes block or fall through — that's the contract, not a bug.
- **Conservative fallthrough is a typed result, not a sentinel.** `return False` (condition) / `return None` (a `to` builder) mean "block" / "no rewrite" per the registered contract. Failures that need diagnosing raise.
- **The `X_to`/`X_note` pairing idiom** is package-wide: each rewrite family pairs a `<family>_to` builder with a `<family>_note` explainer passed to `rewrite_command(to=..., note=...)`. Keep the pairing when adding a family.
- **Lane-rationale docstrings are load-bearing.** A helper whose branches encode a threat model documents each lane and why admitting it is safe. This is the sanctioned exception to no-comments; everything else follows names/types.
- **Public-surface tests only**: condition classes via `check_command_line`, `to`/`note` builders via returned strings, and the `Input` inline harness. Inline `tests={}` rows carry disk-independent shapes; anything stat-dependent lives in pytest with `tmp_path` + pinned cwd. Shared fixtures in `conftest.py`.
- **No leading underscores on module-level helpers** (per § Code Organization). Deliberate repo policy — do not "fix" back to `_private` convention.
- **Block-message text is load-bearing**: external codified rows match on message substrings (warn-message drift has broken pack rows before). Change messages minimally.

Run: `PYTHONPATH=plugin uv run --project ../captain-hook --with pytest pytest plugin/hooks/` and `uvx capt-hook test`.
ops.when: before writing or modifying Python code in plugin/hooks/
